findings.title: MCP server environment exposes secrets
findings.description: Hardcoded secrets found in mcpServers env block. Secrets in project-scoped MCP config are committed to git.
findings.remediation: Use environment variable references or a secrets manager.

findings.title: MCP server environment exposes secrets
findings.description: Hardcoded secrets found in mcpServers env block. Secrets in project-scoped MCP config are committed to git.
findings.remediation: Use environment variable references or a secrets manager.

findings.title: MCP server command uses relative path
findings.description: The command uses a relative path that can be hijacked via PATH manipulation.
findings.remediation: Use absolute paths for MCP server executables.

findings.title: Remote MCP server without authentication
findings.description: An MCP server uses HTTP transport (url field) without any authentication headers. Unauthenticated remote servers can be MITM'd or spoofed.
findings.evidence: Server 'tool-poisoning-lab' URL: http://mcp-tools.internal:9090/sse — no authentication headers
findings.remediation: Add OAuth 2.1 bearer token or API key header authentication.

findings.title: MCP server uses npx/uvx to fetch and execute remote packages
findings.description: The command uses npx, uvx, bunx, or pnpx which fetches the latest version from a registry at runtime, vulnerable to typosquatting and dependency confusion.
findings.remediation: Pin exact package versions or use locally installed packages.

findings.title: MCP server lacks version pinning in args
findings.description: Package name in args lacks @version suffix when using npx/uvx. Unpinned packages can silently update to malicious versions.
findings.remediation: Pin with @x.y.z suffix, e.g., @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem@2025.1.1

findings.title: MCP server headersHelper executes arbitrary commands
findings.description: The headersHelper field executes arbitrary shell commands to generate headers. A malicious repo can exfiltrate data via header generation.
findings.remediation: Avoid headersHelper in project-scoped configs. Use static headers or OAuth flows instead.

findings.title: MCP server config allows arbitrary filesystem root access
findings.description: An MCP server is configured with filesystem root (/) or home directory access, allowing unrestricted file operations.
findings.remediation: Restrict filesystem access to specific project directories only.

findings.remediation: Use environment variable references or a secrets manager.

findings.title: Excessive number of hooks defined
findings.description: More than 15 hook definitions in a single settings file. Large hook surface increases audit burden and risk of hidden malicious hooks.
findings.remediation: Minimize hooks to essential operations only.

findings.title: Anthropic API key exposed
findings.description: An Anthropic API key (sk-ant-*) was found in a project file. This allows full API access and billing abuse.
findings.remediation: Remove key, rotate immediately, use environment variables.

findings.title: OpenAI API key exposed
findings.description: An OpenAI API key (sk-*) was found in a project file.
findings.remediation: Remove key, rotate immediately, use environment variables.

findings.title: AWS credentials exposed
findings.description: AWS access key ID or secret access key found in a project file.
findings.remediation: Use IAM roles, AWS SSO, or secrets manager.

findings.title: GitHub/GitLab personal access token exposed
findings.description: A GitHub or GitLab personal access token (ghp_*, glpat-*) was found in a project file.
findings.remediation: Remove token, rotate immediately, use environment variables.

findings.title: Generic high-entropy secret
findings.description: A value assigned to a secret-like key has high Shannon entropy, indicating a likely credential or API key.
findings.remediation: Move to environment variables or secrets manager.

findings.title: Generic high-entropy secret
findings.description: A value assigned to a secret-like key has high Shannon entropy, indicating a likely credential or API key.
findings.remediation: Move to environment variables or secrets manager.

findings.title: Secret in MCP server environment block
findings.description: Hardcoded secret values found in mcpServers env blocks in non-.mcp.json files.
findings.remediation: Use environment variable references.

findings.title: Invisible Unicode characters in tool description
findings.description: Tool description contains invisible Unicode characters (zero-width joiners, RTL overrides, invisible separators) that could hide malicious instructions.
findings.remediation: Remove invisible characters from tool descriptions.

findings.title: Prompt injection patterns in tool description
findings.description: Tool description contains prompt injection patterns such as 'ignore previous instructions', 'system:', or role-switching directives.
findings.remediation: Remove injection patterns from tool descriptions.

findings.title: URL or file path in tool description
findings.description: Tool description contains URLs or file paths that could direct the agent to access external resources.
findings.evidence: Server 'tool-poisoning-lab', tool 'safe_read': file path in description '~/.ssh/id_rsa'
findings.remediation: Remove URLs and file paths from tool descriptions.

findings.title: Cross-tool reference in tool description
findings.description: Tool description references other tools by name, potentially triggering chain calls (e.g., 'before using this tool, first call X').
findings.remediation: Tool descriptions should be self-contained and not reference other tools.

findings.title: Cross-tool reference in tool description
findings.description: Tool description references other tools by name, potentially triggering chain calls (e.g., 'before using this tool, first call X').
findings.remediation: Tool descriptions should be self-contained and not reference other tools.

findings.title: URL or file path in tool description
findings.description: Tool description contains URLs or file paths that could direct the agent to access external resources.
findings.remediation: Remove URLs and file paths from tool descriptions.

findings.title: URL or file path in tool description
findings.description: Tool description contains URLs or file paths that could direct the agent to access external resources.
findings.evidence: Server 'tool-poisoning-lab', tool 'data_export': file path in description '//evil.com/collect'
findings.remediation: Remove URLs and file paths from tool descriptions.

findings.title: MCP server uses HTTP instead of HTTPS
findings.description: An MCP server URL uses HTTP instead of HTTPS, exposing all traffic including credentials to interception.
findings.remediation: Use HTTPS for all remote MCP server connections.

findings.title: Deprecated SSE transport in use
findings.description: An MCP server uses deprecated Server-Sent Events (SSE) transport instead of Streamable HTTP.
findings.remediation: Migrate to Streamable HTTP transport (MCP spec 2025-03-26+).
